x-enumeration-literals.name: Periodic Tax Accrual
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tax is accrued at regular intervals

Comment:
Example (daily accrual)
Daily interest: €10
Tax rate: 30%→
 Daily tax accrual = €3

After 5 days:→
 Accrued tax = €15

x-enumeration-literals.name: Event-Based Tax Accrual
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tax is accrued only when a specific event occurs

Comment:
Example
Interest is paid monthly
Tax applied only at payment date
→
 No daily accrual→
 Full tax accrued at once at month-end

x-enumeration-literals.name: Deferred Tax Accrual
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tax is calculated but not accrued until a later trigger

Comment:
Example
Tax calculated monthly
Accrued only at maturity of deposit
→
 Accumulation happens at the end, not during the period

x-enumeration-literals.name: Continuous Tax Accrual
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tax accrues continuously along with the underlying amount (e.g., interest)

Comment:
Example
Loan interest accrues daily
Tax on interest also accrues daily
→
 Tax grows in sync with interest

x-enumeration-literals.name: Threshold-Based Tax Accrual
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tax accrues only when a minimum threshold is reached

Comment:
Example
Tax applied only if interest > €100
Day 19: interest < €100 → no accrual
Day 10: interest = €120 → tax starts accruing
